conversations.say: Dee, the protections are now on record. I will say only that my unverified access arrangement overlaps your service-exit gap; it identifies nobody. The choice to enter Morgan’s conference remains yours.
conversations.offer: I will attend as your advocate, stop questions outside the agreed route issue, and protect every uninvolved name.
conversations.request: Consent voluntarily to one structured conference with Morgan, limited to whether our accounts overlap on access and timing—not identity, purpose, or destination.

conversations.say: Morgan, you bought a bounded comparison, not the neighborhood directory. My account and Dee’s establish possible unlogged service access during the gap, but neither identifies the user or connects that user to Vale.
conversations.offer: If Dee consents, I will convene the voluntary conference and identify the precise overlap without naming uninvolved people.
conversations.request: Record the overlap as bounded and the conflict with the manufactured client segment as unresolved; preserve every agreed witness protection.

conversations.say: Celeste, the neighborhood upheld its side: I have offered Dee the protected voluntary meeting and will serve as advocate if Dee consents. Standing safeguards remain the price of continuing cooperation.
conversations.offer: I will help a community-selected assessor specify anonymity, reporting, and audit provisions without receiving witness identities.
conversations.request: Issue the written anti-retaliation, advocate-access, and uninvolved-name protections and preserve the procurement safeguards as standing policy.

public_statement: Here is all the route clarification the neighborhood authorizes: I arranged unlogged access without verifying the person, and that may overlap the service-exit gap. It identifies nobody and does not establish that Vale used the route. Any further comparison is voluntary, protected, and limited. City safeguards and labor-backed anonymity are the negotiated return—not payment for names.

commitments.promise: Conduct one voluntary structured conference only with Dee’s consent; preserve advocate access, no surprise questioning, no payment for identities, no uninvolved names, quotation review, and unresolved disagreements.

Publicly support standing procurement safeguards and broker a protected voluntary local-holder meeting; City Hall provides written anti-retaliation, advocate-access, and uninvolved-identity protections.
Neither party will disclose neighborhood identities or additional route details without mutual consent and negotiated protection.

correction: The overlap between my unverified access arrangement and Dee’s service-exit gap does not identify the user, purpose, destination, or any connection to Vale.
goal_progress: Secured standing City Hall procurement, reporting, audit, anti-retaliation, advocate-access, and uninvolved-identity protections.
Secured labor organizing support for anonymity, consent, attribution, and anti-retaliation.
Preserved the disclosure boundary with Rafi and made Dee’s participation voluntary.
Released only bounded route context needed for comparison, without identifying uninvolved people.
reasoning_summary: Shared human production is established, while later infrastructure and account behavior make a continuing mantle plausible but do not identify a sender or prove autonomous control. Vale’s prior disappearance route and the manufactured segment make voluntary withdrawal more plausible than murder, though the present route remains unverified.
